representative_work: CSP Is Dead, Long Live CSP!; Content Security Problems?; Semantics-Based Analysis of CSP Deployment; Complex Security Policy?
what_it_does: Measures or formalizes CSP effectiveness, deployment, bypassable whitelists, longitudinal adoption, and operator experience.
our_boundary: We do not measure site-wide CSP deployment or synthesize CSPs. We model cross-policy policy-intent drift between explicit source claims, generated policy surfaces, and browser-effective semantics.
paper_use: Motivation and Related Work; explains why CSP-only measurement is not sufficient.

representative_work: We Still Don't Have Secure Cross-Domain Requests
what_it_does: Empirical study of real-world CORS design, implementation, deployment issues, risky trust dependencies, and developer misunderstanding.
our_boundary: We use CORS as one semantic stratum and focus on source-grounded drift/witnesses rather than Internet-scale CORS prevalence.
paper_use: Motivation for request-context semantics and related work.

representative_work: Reining in the Web's Inconsistencies with Site Policy
what_it_does: Formalizes security-policy inconsistencies for cookies, CSP, and HSTS across pages within origins/sites and evaluates at web scale.
our_boundary: We do not propose origin/site deployment policy or crawl 15k sites. We analyze developer-facing intent drift caused by disposition, fallback, rendering context, UA state, and cross-policy composition.
paper_use: Closest-work contrast; must be positioned carefully in Introduction and Related Work.

representative_work: DiffCSP; A Tale of Two Headers; CSP/SOP interaction studies
what_it_does: Finds browser enforcement bugs or inconsistent browser support/interpretation for particular policy mechanisms.
our_boundary: We treat browser specifications and developer documentation as source-grounding and do not hunt browser engine bugs or compare browser implementations.

representative_work: CSPAutoGen and related CSP generation systems
what_it_does: Synthesizes or enforces CSPs for websites from runtime or black-box behavior.
our_boundary: We do not generate deployment policies; we generate minimal semantic witnesses for drift in documented/generated policy surfaces.
paper_use: Related Work contrast for tool contribution.

representative_work: CSP Evaluator; MDN HTTP Observatory; OWASP Secure Headers
what_it_does: Check individual headers, CSP bypass patterns, or security-header configurations.
our_boundary: They are baselines/controls. The paper claim is semantic witness generation for policy-intent drift, not a better grade or checklist.

representative_work: HSTS RFC; hstspreload; HSTS deployment/preload studies
what_it_does: Specifies HSTS UA state and preload eligibility or measures preload/deployment behavior.
our_boundary: We encode HSTS state-transition fragments only where they interact with intent/generation claims and negative controls.

cluster: Cross-origin isolation documentation and web platform evolution
what_it_does: Explains isolation requirements and compatibility tradeoffs for powerful features and embedded resources.
our_boundary: We lower explicit requirements into source-grounded fixture obligations rather than evaluating adoption.
paper_use: Corpus source grounding and motivating examples.

representative_work: Next.js CSP, Helmet, Django SecurityMiddleware, Spring Security headers, Rails CSP, ASP.NET Core CORS, Express cors
what_it_does: Documents how security-policy headers are generated, defaulted, migrated, or constrained by rendering/deployment context.
our_boundary: These are public source units in the corpus; we do not compare framework market share or scan real applications.

representative_work: Software Configuration Engineering in Practice; Empirical Study on Configuration Errors; ConfInLog
what_it_does: Studies configuration constraints, failures, and developer practices across systems.
our_boundary: BEP-IR makes browser-policy configuration semantics explicit for one security-critical domain.

cluster: Secure code review and security feature engineering
representative_work: Secure code review studies; ICSE security feature engineering work
what_it_does: Studies how developers review, maintain, and engineer security-relevant features.
our_boundary: Our validation is source-grounded and deterministic; we avoid claiming human workflow prevalence without additional study.

representative_work: Reproducibility and Replicability in Science; ICSE artifact/open science studies
what_it_does: Defines reproducibility concepts and reports challenges in executing research artifacts.
our_boundary: We design the artifact to be CPU-native and transparent about unavailable external baselines.

what_it_does: Introduces systematic input minimization to isolate failure-inducing differences.
our_boundary: Our minimizer is domain-specific and one-deletion minimal over policy witnesses, not a general delta-debugging theorem.

representative_work: Thematic analysis; qualitative methods in SE; grounded theory guidance
what_it_does: Provides coding, validity, and qualitative-method guidance.
our_boundary: Current corpus validation is deterministic audit, not human inter-rater agreement; use method references only for coding protocol structure.
